A.rationale: Correct implementation and verified via testing. Tested composition with --upper. Very helpful CLI buffering analysis.

B.rationale: Correct implementation and verified via testing, but verbose and includes unnecessary discussion and follow-up questions.

C.rationale: Highly concise and correct implementation. Tested non-mutation, but did not test interaction/composition with --upper.

notes: A and C both implemented the feature well, but A is best because it tested flag composition with --upper and provided valuable edge case analysis.
